title: Q: Issue with compiling and running Verilator SystemC tests
body:
Hi, how should we `configure`/`install` Verilator to be able to compile and run SystemC tests?

I used the following command to configure Verilator
```
sudo ./configure --prefix=/work/verilator/`git describe | sed "s/verilator_//"` CXX="clang++ -std=c++14" LINK=clang++
```
During the config, I get the following output
```
checking whether SystemC is found (in system path)... no
```
But, SystemC variables are set
```
env | grep SYSTEMC

SYSTEMC_HOME=/work/systemc-2.3.3
SYSTEMC_LIBDIR=/work/systemc-2.3.3/lib-linux64
SYSTEMC_INCLUDE=/work/systemc-2.3.3/include
```
`make` and `make test` run without any problem.

If I try to execute some SystemC test I get the following warning:
```
-Skip: dist/t_var_sc_bv: scenario 'dist' not enabled for test
dist/t_var_sc_bv: -Skip: Skip: scenario 'dist' not enabled for test
==SUMMARY: Passed 0  Failed 0  Unsup 0  Time 0:00
======================================================================
vlt/t_var_sc_bv: ==================================================
-Skip: vlt/t_var_sc_bv: Test requires SystemC; ignore error since not installed
```

Should I add some flag during the `configure` (i.e. SystemC path as `CXX` or something like that) or I did some other step wrong?


Thank you

-Skip: dist/t_var_sc_bv: scenario 'dist' not enabled for test
dist/t_var_sc_bv: -Skip: Skip: scenario 'dist' not enabled for test
==SUMMARY: Passed 0  Failed 0  Unsup 0  Time 0:00
